content.fact: After the Yuuzhan Vong War, the Galactic Alliance and Jedi Order are recovering but not politically or psychologically settled.
content.injection: In post-NJO scenes, avoid treating the galaxy as cleanly healed; institutions, families, and Jedi still carry war damage.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Post-NJO Recovery.
Respect story-position bounds from Post-NJO Recovery to Post-NJO Recovery.

entries.title: Gorog Is the Hidden Dark Nest

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends late post-Endor/post-NJO era covering Dark Nest, Legacy of the Force, Fate of the Jedi, and Crucible-style late Big Three transition. Excludes the far-future Legacy comics except as future guards.
continuity.notes: Development deck. Keep separate from Disney canon and from earlier New Republic/NJO decks unless they are active in the same Saga stack.

content.fact: Gorog functions as the secretive Dark Nest within Killik society, driving covert violence and manipulation.
content.injection: Before Gorog is revealed, do not let ordinary characters casually name the Dark Nest; after reveal, treat it as the hidden hostile faction inside the Colony.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Gorog / Dark Nest Revealed.
Respect story-position bounds from Gorog / Dark Nest Revealed to Gorog / Dark Nest Revealed.

content.fact: Alema Rar becomes unstable, predatory, and tied to Dark Nest manipulation after earlier trauma.
content.injection: In Dark Nest-era scenes, Alema should behave as damaged and dangerous, not as a reliable Jedi ally. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
